title: [InstCombine] Optimistically allow multiple shufflevector uses in foldOpPhi
summary:
The developers generalized an optimization pattern by extending `foldOpIntoPhi` to handle shufflevector instructions with multiple identical users. Originally, the function only supported folding operations into a PHI node when the PHI had a single use. The generalization allows folding when all users of the PHI are identical shufflevector operations, enabling the transformation to replace all such users simultaneously.

Key changes:
1. Added an `AllowMultipleUses` parameter to `foldOpIntoPhi`, defaulting to false for backward compatibility.
2. Modified the shufflevector combine logic to pass `true` for this parameter, permitting the fold when all PHI users are identical shuffles.
3. Updated the logic to check for identical users across all PHI operands, ensuring safety before proceeding.
4. Adjusted the replacement logic to handle multiple identical users collectively, replacing each with the new PHI node.

This approach transforms a single-case optimization into a reusable pattern applicable to any shufflevector that deinterleaves a PHI node, improving code generation for vectorized loops and other patterns where the same shuffle is applied to a PHI in multiple places.
